split_suggestion: Split into (1) WT1 loss-of-function as a molecular event and (2) blocked nephron progenitor differentiation as the cellular consequence
reasoning: The defining claim is loss of a transcription factor (molecular-scale gene product inactivation), but the node simultaneously asserts a developmental cellular outcome (failed differentiation), making the molecular primary defensible but contested.
The node centers on loss of a specific protein component of the beta-catenin destruction complex enabling Wnt pathway derepression — a protein-scale molecular regulatory event.
Exon 3 gain-of-function mutation stabilizes beta-catenin protein and activates canonical Wnt signaling — a prototypical molecular activity node centered on a specific gene product's altered function.

Aberrant beta-catenin pathway signaling is pathway-level molecular activity acting within nephrogenic progenitor cells to sustain an undifferentiated state — the signaling cascade itself is molecular but the framing of cell-state maintenance pulls toward cellular.

The node describes failure of a specific cell population (nephrogenic rests / blastemal cells) to undergo terminal differentiation into mature nephrons — a canonical cell-intrinsic developmental process.
Biallelic IGF2 expression arising from epigenetic loss of imprinting at 11p15 is an epigenetic-regulatory molecular event producing excess growth factor signaling.

split_suggestion: Split into (1) DROSHA loss-of-function and (2) DICER1 loss-of-function, as two distinct miRNA biogenesis enzyme mutations that may have partially distinct target profiles
reasoning: DROSHA and DICER1 mutations disrupt the molecular miRNA biogenesis machinery (RNA processing enzymes), making this a molecular-scale enzymatic defect — the downstream cellular effects are consequential rather than the defining claim.

SIX1/SIX2 hotspot mutations reinforce a cell-intrinsic transcriptional program maintaining progenitor identity and proliferative capacity — the unit of description is the self-renewing progenitor cell and its behavioral state.

proposes_new_kind: CELLULAR_STATE — this node describes an emergent maintained condition of a cell population (high proliferative capacity plus failed differentiation) rather than an ongoing process; the process-centric enum fits poorly
reasoning: Multiple upstream drivers converge on persistence of a cell population state rather than a discrete ongoing process — CELLULAR_PROCESS is the least-bad fit but the node is fundamentally a state description.
Expansion of embryonal renal progenitors into overt nephroblastoma is neoplastic growth at the kidney organ level — a tissue/organ-scale process with an anatomical location anchor.

split_suggestion: Split into (1) TP53 loss-of-function (molecular: impaired DNA repair and apoptosis signaling) and (2) anaplastic tumor progression (tissue: aggressive clonal expansion with nuclear atypia)
reasoning: The node bundles a molecular driver (TP53 dysfunction affecting DNA repair and apoptosis pathways) with a tissue-level outcome (anaplastic tumor evolution), making either scale defensible — the title 'Progression' tilts toward tissue-level.
